## Threads

<!--
Threads is build-in-public.
Use this section for light progress, background, trial-and-error, or a small insight / next step.
These lines may be auto-posted after merge to main, so only keep this section for user-visible changes with clear value.

Write two short paragraphs in this order:
- first paragraph: English
- second paragraph: Japanese

Write them as normal sentences:
- not the PR title rewritten
- human-readable, not PR / commit jargon
- include what changed
- include why it is useful
- include a short hint of what Cloud Weave is when possible
- keep it to 2-4 short sentences total
- reduce jargon
- keep the combined English + Japanese post within 500 characters total

Good shape:
- progress
- background / why
- small insight or next step if it still fits in one post

Keep both paragraphs short enough for one Threads post:
Cloud Weave now supports uploads, so you can drag files in and let the app choose the right storage.

Cloud Weave にアップロード機能を追加し、保存先を毎回考えずにファイルを入れられるようにしました。

If this section exists, the workflow will post it to Threads after merge to main.
Delete the whole section when:
- the change is internal only
- the value is not obvious to users
- you do not want a Threads post
-->

## X

<!--
X is manual-only. Nothing in this section is auto-posted.
Use this as a draft area for a stronger outward-facing post for the main account.
This should read more like a representative product update than a build log.

Write two short paragraphs in this order:
- first paragraph: English
- second paragraph: Japanese

Prefer 2-4 short sentences total with this shape:
- what changed
- why it helps
- one design insight / learning / product direction
- keep the combined English + Japanese draft within 280 characters total

Always try to make these clear:
- what Cloud Weave is
- what became easier for the user
- what is exciting or notable about this change

Avoid:
- PR title paraphrases
- in-progress notes or trial-and-error updates
- internal implementation detail lists
- developer-only wording with no user value

Best for:
- big progress
- demos
- design learnings
- release-like milestones

Good shape:
- fact
- value
- learning

Example:
Cloud Weave now supports uploads.
You can drag files in or browse from your computer, and the app routes them to the right cloud storage.
The goal is to reduce the mental overhead of deciding where files should go.

Cloud Weave にアップロード機能を追加しました。
ファイルをドラッグ&ドロップしたり選択したりすると、アプリが適切なクラウド保存先に振り分けます。
毎回どこに置くか考えなくていい体験を目指しています。
-->
